input.background: An environmental pressure group contacted backbench MPs from two political parties (Beige and Grey, neither of which were in government) just before and immediately after a general election. They asked for their views on environmental issues, and the responses were analyzed using content analysis. The number of positive words in each politician's statements was recorded. Assume that each MP was randomly selected before the election and, if re-elected, was omitted from the random survey after the election, ensuring the data are independent.
input.question: Carry out a two-way analysis of variance using ranks to determine whether there is an effect of party, timing (before or after the general election), or an interaction between the two.

meta_info.chapter: DIFFERENCES BETWEEN MORE THAN TWO SAMPLES
